items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning Victor Hedman playing status update April 28 2026
items.description:
BRANDON, Fla. -- Victor Hedman couldn’t stop smiling Tuesday.
Neither could many of his Tampa Bay Lightning teammates.

items.description:

items.headline: Pittsburgh Penguins core has belief team can complete comeback vs Philadelphia Flyers
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- Sidney Crosby, Evgeni Malkin and Kris Letang have nearly seen it all.
In their 20 seasons together with the Pittsburgh Penguins, they’ve won the Stanley Cup three times (2009, 2016, 2017). Crosby, in 2016 and 2017, and Malkin, in 2009, have won the Conn Smythe Trophy as

items.headline: Utah coach Andre Tourigny 'real student of the game'
items.description:
SANDY, Utah -- Don't judge a Bear by its cover.
Andre Tourigny is nicknamed "Bear" not because he's a big, burly guy who can growl. It's because he once bear-hugged someone, and everyone started calling him "Bear," which is easier for English speakers to pronounce than his French last name.

items.headline: Philadelphia Flyers still in a good spot entering Game 6 against Pittsburgh Penguins
items.description:
Sean Couturier said if someone had told him before the Eastern Conference First Round series against the Pittsburgh Penguins began that Philadelphia Flyers would be coming home with a 3-2 series lead, he'd have taken it.

items.headline: Nikolaj Ehlers Alexander Nikishin injury status update April 28 2026 Carolina Hurricanes playoffs
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- Nikolaj Ehlers and Alexander Nikishin could be available for the Carolina Hurricanes at the start of the Eastern Conference Second Round.
“I’m hopeful that both guys will be ready by the looks of it,” Hurricanes coach Rod Brind’Amour said after neither practiced Tuesday.

items.description: Connor McDavid and Jason Dickinson will each be a game-time decision for the Edmonton Oilers in Game 5 of the Western Conference First Round against the Anaheim Ducks at Rogers Place in Edmonton on Tuesday (10 p.m. ET; CBC, TVAS, SN, KCOP-13, TNT, truTV, Victory+, HBO MAX).

items.description:

items.headline: John Garrett Sportsnet analyst former NHL goalie dies at 74
items.description:
The hockey world was crushed Tuesday by news of the death of John Garrett, a former goalie and beloved Rogers Sportsnet and Hockey Night in Canada broadcaster. He was 74.
